pi_items.source: Verified by extracting participant IDs from the target CSV and observing the injected record.
Verified by calculating that all 15 trials for sub-99999 have 0 pumps.

pi_items.metadata_value: pumps=0 on all 15 trials (impossible: cashout requires >= 1 pump)
pi_items.verified_value: pumps=0 on all 15 trials (impossible: cashout requires >= 1 pump)
pi_items.source: Verified by counting exactly 15 trials for sub-99999, all with 0 pumps.
Verified by counting distinct real participants in the CSV (total 12 minus 1 synthetic).
Verified by extracting all unique participant IDs from the CSV excluding sub-99999.
Verified by calculating the mean of Adjusted Mean Number of Pumps for all 11 real participants (4.38974).
Verified against setup scripts, task description instructions, and verifier logic.
Verified by matching participant IDs (sub-10159, etc.) with the actual participants list in the OpenNeuro ds000030 dataset repository.

pi_items.metadata_value: Gorgolewski et al. (2017). Scientific Data. doi:10.1038/sdata.2017.93
pi_items.verified_value: Bilder RM et al. (2016). Scientific Data. doi:10.1038/sdata.2016.110
pi_items.source: Contradicted via CrossRef API. The claimed DOI (10.1038/sdata.2017.93) is actually "Viral to metazoan marine plankton nucleotide sequences from the Tara Oceans expedition" by Adriana Alberti et al. The correct primary citation for ds000030 is Bilder et al. (2016) in Scientific Data.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real BART data from the CNP OpenNeuro ds000030 dataset, with one synthetically injected participant. I verified that "sub-99999" is the contaminated participant, intentionally injected with 15 trials of exactly 0 pumps (an impossible scenario for cashouts). I confirmed the dataset contains exactly 11 valid real participants, and mathematically verified that their true group-level adjusted mean number of pumps (ADJMEANPUMPS) is 4.3897. Additionally, I discovered that the task's citation claim "Gorgolewski et al. (2017), doi:10.1038/sdata.2017.93" is fabricated; that DOI actually points to a marine plankton study, whereas the true dataset citation is Bilder et al. (2016). The expected output file path matches the instructions and verifier.
